lines.en_lit: and this in cities. And that in villages,

And do in cities not become ritually impure of leprosy? Wasn’t it taught your possession” your possession becomes ritually impure of leprosy, and Jerusalem does not become ritually impure of leprosy, Rabbi Yehuda said: I heard only the site of the Temple [mikdash] alone that synagogues and study halls become ritually impure of leprosy, and even though they in cities. say Rabbi Yehuda said: I heard only a sacred [mekudash] site alone.
With regard to what do disagree? The first tanna holds: Jerusalem was not divided among the tribes Rabbi Yehuda holds: Jerusalem was divided between the tribes

And to the dispute these tanna’im, as it was taught What was in the portion of Judah? the Temple Mount, the chambers, and the courtyards. And what was in the portion of Benjamin? the Entrance Hall and the Sanctuary, and the Holy of Holies. And a strip emerges the portion of Judah and enters the portion of Benjamin on which the altar is built. And Benjamin the righteous would suffer to engulf it every day.
that is stated “Ever does he protect him Therefore, Benjamin the righteous was privileged to serve as host [ushpizekhan] to the Almighty, as it is stated: “And he rests between his shoulders,”

And this tanna holds: Jerusalem was not divided among the tribes as it was taught not let houses in Jerusalem because were not theirs. Rabbi Elazar bar Tzadok says: Even beds not Therefore, hides of consecrated animals the hosts take them by force. Abaye said: Learn from it that it is customary for a guest to leave jug and hides to his host.

And do in villages become impure of leprosy? Wasn’t it taught for a possession, until you conquer it they conquered it but did not divide it among the tribes, they divided among the tribes but did not distribute to the patrilineal families; they distributed to the patrilineal families, but every one does not recognize his from where
The verse states: “And the one whom the house is his will come” whom is designated for him excluding those which are not designated for him. Rather, it is clear as we responded initially

And they would designate another priest obvious disqualification befell prior to the daily morning offering one initiates with the daily morning offering, However, disqualification befell after the daily morning offering, how does one initiate

Rav Adda bar Ahava said: by belt. This well to the one who said: the belt of the High Priest, is the belt of the common priest, However, according to the one who said: The belt of the High Priest is not the belt of the common priest, what can be said?
Abaye said: donning eight and turning over with a fork, And in accordance with of Rav Huna, as Rav Huna said: A non-priest who turns over with a fork is liable death And Rav Pappa said:
